name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist and materials scientist with 6+ years of experience developing and characterizing polymer and thin-film materials, establishing structure-property-processing relationships, and driving process optimization through DOE and multi-technique analysis. Expertise spans FT-IR, thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), ellipsometry, QCM-D, GPC, and hypothesis-driven materials problem solving for high-performance polymer systems.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead process development for novel bio-based polymer systems, applying DOE to identify critical parameters, improve yield and product quality, and establish reproducible process windows for scale-up.
Translate material performance targets into experimental plans and analytical workflows, using thermal and spectroscopic data to guide material and process decisions.

Established structure-property relationships in functional organic material systems through FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, electrochemical, TGA, and DSC characterization, guiding materials design and performance optimization.

Diagnosed degradation and interface-instability mechanisms in thin-film material systems through hypothesis-driven experiments, then proposed design and process mitigations to improve durability and performance.

Co-led AFOSR-funded development of molecular thin-film coatings for optical hardware, designing surface-preparation and CVD deposition strategies to achieve targeted film morphology, adhesion, and device performance.
Developed and optimized polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE and in-situ QCM-D monitoring, establishing process controls that improved coating reproducibility and growth uniformity.

Managed a 15-member research laboratory operating multiple analytical instruments and parallel materials projects, implemented equipment tracking/calibration systems, and improved instrument uptime by 30%.
Authored SOPs and coordinated technical workflows across concurrent projects with tight quality and timeline requirements.

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design to improve reproducibility, yield, and material quality.

projects.title: Thin-Film Coatings for Optical Hardware
projects.description: Co-led development of high-performance molecular thin-film coatings, tuning deposition and surface-preparation conditions to meet optical and materials-performance targets.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Polymer & Materials Characterization: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, ellipsometry, TGA, DSC, QCM-D, GPC, NMR, spectroelectrochemistry
skills.Materials Expertise: Polymer systems, thin films, coatings, surface functionalization, structure-property-processing relationships, degradation analysis
skills.Process & Data: DOE, SPC, statistical data analysis, process optimization, root cause analysis, technical documentation
skills.Execution: Multi-project management, cross-functional communication, stakeholder updates, experimental planning

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
